reasoning: The Lean signature quantifies over x in I ⊓ J (which is the intersection of ideals in Mathlib) and asserts that its image under Ideal.Quotient.mk (I*J) is nilpotent — precisely the informal claim. Using CommRing matches Artin's standard commutative-ring setting, where the product ideal IJ is defined. The subtype coercion from ↑(I ⊓ J) to R works with Ideal.Quotient.mk, and the free variable x corresponds to "any element". No hypotheses are added or dropped relative to the informal statement, and the conclusion (IsNilpotent) matches exactly.
